title: Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Battle of Coruscant}} {{Battle |prev=[[Battle of Foerost (Great Sith War)|Battle of Foerost]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 1]] |conc= |next=[[Trial of Ulic Qel-Droma]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 3]] |image=[[File:BattleOfCoruscant1-TSW2.jpg]] |name=Battle of Coruscant |conflict=*[[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' **[[Krath Holy Crusade]] |date=[[3996 BBY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |place=[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]] |result=*[[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|Sith Empire]] withdrawal *[[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] captured by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *Coruscant ravaged''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' |side1=*Galactic Republic *[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] |side2=*Sith Empire **[[Krath/Legends|Krath]] **[[Mandalorian Crusaders]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=*[[Minister of Defense (Republic)|Minister of Defense]]{{CSWECite|II|372|Netus}} [[Netus]] *[[Republic command center]] [[Unidentified commander (Coruscant's war room)|commander]]{{CSWECite|III|302|Vento system}} *[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] *Jedi Master [[Thon]][[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] |commanders2=*[[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] Ulic Qel-Droma *[[Aleema Keto]] *[[Mandalore the Indomitable]] |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*2 Jedi Masters *7 [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] *Republic [[Atmosphere/Legends|aerial]] forces *[[Republic soldier]]s *[[Laser cannon/Legends|Laser cannon]] emplacements *[[Skiff/Legends|Republic repulsorcraft]] |forces2=*1 [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] *1 [[Sith sorcerer|Sith sorceress]] *Stolen Republic [[Warship/Legends|warships]] **[[Command ship/Legends|Command ships]] *[[CX-133 Chaos fighter]]s *[[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]] *At least 1 phalanx of [[Tetan/Legends|Tetan]] ground [[Soldier/Legends|troops]] *100+ Mandalorian Crusaders *[[Skiff/Legends|Krath hovercraft]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*Multiple soldiers *At least 2 [[Laser/Legends|laser]] [[Cannon/Legends|cannons]] |casual2=*1 Sith Lord captured *At least 1 warship *At least 1 Basilisk [[Battle droid/Legends|war droid]] *2 Basilisk [[Droid/Legends|droids]] captured *Multiple Tetan soldiers *Multiple Mandalorian Crusaders |casual3= |casual4= |civilian=Multiple damaged buildings }} {{Quote|We caught Coruscant completely by surprise—exactly as you said.|Mandalore the Indomitable, to Ulic Qel-Droma|}} The '''Battle of Coruscant''' was a surprise attack launched in the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] of [[3996 BBY]] by the [[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|Sith Empire]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. After [[Duel in the Iron Citadel (Exar Kun)|joining forces]] with the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] in [[3997 BBY]], the fellow fallen [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] led the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] [[darksider]] cult on a [[Krath Holy Crusade|galactic conquest]]. When Qel-Droma later [[Duel on Kuar|gained]] the allegiance of the [[Mandalorian Crusaders]] under the [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|command]] of [[Mandalore the Indomitable]] as well, the former, the latter, and Qel-Droma's lover, co-leader of the Krath [[Aleema Keto]], all came to believe that the [[Time/Legends|time]] was right for a surprise attack on Coruscant with the goal of eliminating any further Republic resistance to a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] domination of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Launching a [[Battle of Foerost (Great Sith War)|raid]] on the [[Foerost Shipyards]] during which Qel-Droma's forces captured nearly three hundred of the Republic's newest [[Warship/Legends|warships]] and diverted the main [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic fleet's]] attention to the [[Kemplex Nine]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]], they then traveled to Coruscant. At the time, a group of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] led by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] also had arrived on the planet in order to petition the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] regarding the ongoing Sith attacks on Republic assets. When the Jedi were still on the landing platform of their [[Vodo-Siosk Baas's heavy courier|starship]], the Krath [[Starship/Legends|warships]] arrived in Coruscant's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] and initiated a massive attack on the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|city-world]]. The Jedi and Republic [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] present on Coruscant were forced to defend the [[Galactic City/Legends|city]] against strafing runs of Krath [[CX-133 Chaos fighter|starfighters]] and [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droid]]–riding [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]]. Meanwhile, Qel-Droma and Keto led a phalanx of [[Tetan/Legends|Tetan]] ground troops on an attempt to capture the [[Republic command center]] and therefore disrupt the Republic's military operations, and Mandalore and [[Gender/Legends|his]] soldiers captured new weapons that were being produced at a nearby [[Unidentified weapons depot|weapons depot]]. Baas's Jedi group, sensing Qel-Droma's presence, eventually also advanced on the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] center, where the former Jedi was attempting to force the war room's [[Unidentified commander (Coruscant's war room)|commander]] to order Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] to jump to an identical set of coordinates and thus be destroyed. At that point, Keto, who had her own designs for Krath leadership, abandoned Qel-Droma and withdrew the Krath and Mandalorian forces from Coruscant, retreating to the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]]. Abandoned and outnumbered by the Jedi, Qel-Droma attempted to resist but was soon arrested by them and turned over to the Republic to face [[Trial of Ulic Qel-Droma|trial]] for his actions. ==Prelude== ===Sith strategy=== {{Quote|I've got the ''codes''. The ships are ours. We can leave at once for ''Coruscant''.|Mandalore the Indomitable, to Ulic Qel-Droma|}} [[File:KrathFleet-TSW3.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Krath obtained a fleet of warships that Ulic Qel-Droma planned to use in an attack on Coruscant.]] In [[3997 BBY]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords of the Sith]] and former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] and [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] [[Duel in the Iron Citadel (Exar Kun)|joined forces]], leading to the establishment of a new [[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|Sith Empire]] and the beginning of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and its protector organization, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. While Kun was attempting to convert other [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] to the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] cause, Qel-Droma led the military arm of the empire's campaign, leading the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] [[darksider]] cult on a [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Krath Holy Crusade|conquest]]. In [[3996 BBY]], Qel-Droma [[Duel on Kuar|gained]] the allegiance of [[Mandalore the Indomitable]], the [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|leader]] of the [[Mandalorian Crusaders]]. The former Jedi then led the Krath and the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] on a series of raids on [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic military]] outposts and [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]], including the [[Foerost Shipyards]], where they stole nearly three hundred{{CSWECite|I|284|Foerost}} of the Republic's newest [[Warship/Legends|warships]]. By that point, Qel-Droma, Mandalore, and Qel-Droma's lover, the [[Sith sorcerer|Sith sorceress]] and co-leader of the Krath [[Aleema Keto]]—who was manipulating the former Jedi{{FactFile|20|WAR 1-4|The Sith War}}—all believed that the [[Time/Legends|time]] was right for an attempt to conquer [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]],''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'' the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of the Republic,''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' and take down the seat of the Republic government, thus eliminating further resistance to Sith domination of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. To that end, during the [[Battle of Foerost (Great Sith War)|raid]] at [[Foerost/Legends|Foerost]], Qel-Droma intentionally allowed the shipyards' automatic security systems to record [[Gender/Legends|his]] claim that the Krath forces would next target the [[Kemplex Nine]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]]—a ruse that led the Republic to divert its main [[Republic Navy/Legends|fleet]] away from Coruscant and leave the capital with minimal defenses. Qel-Droma believed that his closest former friends among the Jedi—his brother, [[Cay Qel-Droma|Cay]], and his former romantic interest,[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 6|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 6]] [[Nomi Sunrider]]—would not be on Coruscant at the time of the attack and that the operation would catch the Republic completely by surprise. ===Meetings and arguments=== {{Dialogue|Qel-Droma|I am ''attacking Coruscant'' at once. The ''Republic fleet'' has been alerted to our ''presence'' here. We have led them to expect more terror-attacks on ''shipyards'' and ''military'' outposts. Therefore, the correct move is to attack Coruscant…''at once''.|Kun|Fool! You'll jeopardize my whole plan! We must build ''slowly''… add to the forces… then we will attack ''together''.|Mandalore|I don't know who ''you'' are, but Ulic and I can handle Coruscant.|attr=Ulic Qel-Droma, Exar Kun, and Mandalore the Indomitable|src=}} [[File:MandaloreQelDromaKun-TSW1.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Mandalore the Indomitable (left) and Ulic Qel-Droma (center) disagreed with Exar Kun (hologram; right) on the planned attack.]] When Kun contacted Qel-Droma while the latter was at the Foerost Shipyards, the former overheard the Mandalore affirming to Qel-Droma their readiness for the attack on Coruscant—an operation of which Qel-Droma had not informed Kun and of which the latter disapproved, believing that they instead needed to bide their time and further consolidate their forces. Qel-Droma, however, believed in the superiority of his own acumen over that of Kun and insisted that the planned attack was strategically sound, leading his [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]] to refrain from voicing any further objections to his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice's]] plan. Shortly afterward, when the Jedi Order learned that a rogue Jedi had possibly been involved in the attacks on the Republic targets, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] led a group of Knights—Sunrider, Cay, [[Tott Doneeta]], [[Sylvar]], [[Shoaneb Culu]], [[Dace Diath]], and [[Qrrrl Toq]]—to Coruscant in order to petition the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] to allow the Order to deal with the renegade. The Jedi were met at their [[Vodo-Siosk Baas's heavy courier|starship's]] landing platform by the Republic [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]]{{DB|character|ulicqeldroma|Qel-Droma, Ulic}} [[Sidrona]] and the [[Minister of Defense (Republic)|Republic Minister of Defense]] [[Netus]]. During the meeting, Netus received a message confirming that Qel-Droma was indeed responsible for the Foerost attack, upon which Baas, suspecting that Qel-Droma was cooperating with Kun, expressed his intent to confront them both in an attempt to stop the conflict. ==The battle== ===Surprise attack=== {{Dialogue|Culu|There is a sudden disturbance in the Force… I see an attack force coming out of hyperspace.|Sunrider|It's Ulic!|Netus|Summon our defenses… whatever we've got left!|attr=Shoaneb Culu, Nomi Sunrider, and Netus|src=}} [[File:BattleOfCoruscant2-TSW2.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The massive battle ravaged the surface of Coruscant.]] At that point, Culu experienced a sudden [[Disturbance in the Force/Legends|disturbance in the Force]], [[Force sense/Legends|sensing]] the Krath attack force emerging out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] over Coruscant. Moments later, the warships the Krath had stolen from the Republic entered the planet's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], disgorging [[CX-133 Chaos fighter]]s that began firing at the [[Galactic City/Legends|Republic City's]] buildings and blew up the Jedi [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. Sunrider immediately realized that they were being attacked by Qel-Droma, and Netus ordered a subordinate to summon whatever defenses were at Coruscant's disposal. The Krath initiated a massive battle over Coruscant, attempting to occupy the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|city-world]].[[Star Wars: Force and Destiny Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Core Rulebook]] The Krath warships bombarded buildings, while the Chaos [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] and Mandalorians riding [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]] targeted the [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] emplacements and Coruscant defense teams of [[Republic soldier]]s using [[Rifle/Legends|rifles]] to fend off the attack from the ground. Although Netus soon claimed that the [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] had the situation under control, they had in fact had little experience fighting the Basilisk [[Battle droid/Legends|droid]]–mounted Mandalorians, and at least two [[Cannon/Legends|cannons]] were destroyed by the enemy forces. In turn, at least one warship was brought down crashing during the combat, which also saw the participation of Republic aerial forces and a model of four-[[Wing (technology)|winged]] starfighters. ===Jedi in action=== {{Quote|Master… we've got to get you under cover!"''
''"What is important is that we get to the Republic command center. I have seen that the ''war room'' is where this battle will be decided.|Nomi Sunrider and Vodo-Siosk Baas|}} [[File:JediVsMandalorians-TSW2.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Jedi Knights facing Mandalorian Crusaders on the way to the Republic command center]] In addition, Baas's group of Jedi, which was eventually joined by Jedi Master [[Thon]], also fought in defense of the Republic's capital, utilizing both their [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-using]] and [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] skills. When a Basilisk [[Droid/Legends|droid]] that had destroyed a nearby [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] emplacement returned for another strafing run, Cay and Doneeta attempted to help a soldier activate another cannon, an old unit that was still carrying a charge from a recent [[War Day]] celebration. Meanwhile, Sylvar and Diath used their [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] to bring the droid down. The Jedi Knights and the [[Sword/Legends|sword]]-and-[[Pistol/Legends|pistol]]-wielding Netus attempted to escort Baas to safety, but the Jedi Master, [[Farsight/Legends|foreseeing]] that the battle would be decided in the [[Republic command center]], insisted that the group head there. The Jedi, accompanied by Netus, Sidrona, and a Republic soldier, then boarded a [[Skiff/Legends|repulsorcraft vehicle]] that transported them to the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] center's vicinity. There, the group was intercepted by a unit of Mandalorians armed with ranged weapons, [[Mythosaur axe/Legends|mythosaur axes]], and bladed [[Polearm/Legends|polearms]], leading to a skirmish that delayed the Jedi's progress. ===Stage Two=== {{Quote|We'll move at once on the Republic command center. I want Aleema's illusions, I want a phalanx of Tetan ground troops… and I want a hundred of your best, Mandalore.|Ulic Qel-Droma|}} While the Krath advance teams were being deployed, Qel-Droma, Keto, and Mandalore observed the course of the battle via [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] representation aboard one of their orbiting [[Command ship/Legends|command ships]]. The subsequent second stage of Qel-Droma's battle plan involved capturing the Republic command center, thereby disrupting the operations of the Republic's military command chain. The fallen Jedi assigned a phalanx of [[Tetan/Legends|Tetan]] ground troops and a hundred of the best Mandalorian soldiers to the task. Mandalore himself, having been informed by his [[Spy/Legends|spies]] that the Republic was producing new weaponry at a [[Unidentified weapons depot|weapons depot]] situated near the command center, requested permission from Qel-Droma to capture the technology. The latter felt the weapons would be redundant in the face of their inevitable swift victory but was manipulated by Keto into granting the Mandalorian his wish. [[File:QelDromaAdvance-TSW2.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Ulic Qel-Droma leading the Krath advance toward the command center]] The trio subsequently traveled to Coruscant's surface, where Qel-Droma led a Krath charge on the command center. Having blown a hole in the wall of a building, the Krath soldiers fought Republic forces, with the former Jedi fighting at close quarters with his lightsaber while Keto, staying back aboard a hovercraft with several Tetan troops, supported the attack by casting [[Mind trick/Legends|illusions]] on the Republic soldiers that led them to believe their own weapons had turned into [[Snake/Legends|serpents]]. When the fighting ended, leaving multiple Republic and Krath soldiers [[Death/Legends|dead]], the attackers advanced into the command center itself. Meanwhile, Mandalore and his forces breached the nearby weapons depot, killing its Republic defenders and capturing the powerful [[MX]] [[Laser/Legends|laser]] cannon,{{CSWECite|II|346|MX}} but not before Keto had relayed orders for them to be prepared to withdraw at a moment's notice. The Jedi group led by Baas had by that point cleared a way to the command center, repelling Chaos fighter attacks and even capturing a pair of Basilisk droids. Sunrider sensed Qel-Droma and Keto's presence in the structure, and the group of Jedi broke out in a sprint toward their target. In the Republic's war room, Qel-Droma ordered the [[Unidentified commander (Coruscant's war room)|commander]] present to instruct the Republic's [[Admiral/Legends|admirals]] to deploy all of their ships to an identical set of coordinates in the [[Vento system/Legends|Vento system]], with the intention of having them all collide with and destroy each other upon emerging from hyperspace. When the [[Commander/Legends|commander]] refused the order, Qel-Droma subjected him to a [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force grip]] in order to force him to comply. ===Betrayal and capture=== {{Quote|Tell Mandalore we are pulling back. Immediately. Have him rejoin us at the orbiting command ships. It is time for Ulic Qel-Droma to deal with his own kind.|Aleema Keto, to a Krath subordinate|}} Upon noticing via [[Electrobinoculars/Legends|binoculars]] the Jedi approaching the command center, however, Keto, who felt that the newly-acquired allegiance of the Mandalorians rendered both Qel-Droma and Kun unneeded as means to achieve power, finally saw an opportunity to reassert her dominance over the Krath{{CSWECite|II|213|Keto, Aleema}} by abandoning Qel-Droma to his former friends. Keto ordered her [[Unidentified Krath commander|subordinate soldier]] to remotely instruct Mandalore to immediately retreat and rendezvous with her aboard the Krath command ships. The order surprised the Mandalorian leader, who requested to speak with Qel-Droma personally, only to be told by the Krath soldier that the fallen Jedi had been killed and that the Republic fleet was approaching to vanquish the attackers. The Krath further relayed that Keto wished for Mandalore to take her former lover's place as her [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], at which point the Mandalorian grudgingly complied with the orders, telling his troops to take from the weapons depot whatever plunder they could and then pull out. [[File:QelDromaVsJedi-TSW2.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Ulic Qel-Droma faces Vodo-Siosk Baas and his group of Jedi Knights.]] Seeing the Krath and Mandalorian forces withdraw from the ravaged world surprised Netus, but the Jedi group led by Baas, united in its determination to stop Qel-Droma, nevertheless entered the command center. There, Cay called out to his brother, and Qel-Droma quickly became angered at the unexpected presence of his former Jedi friends. The Krath leader drew his weapon in preparation for an outmatched battle against the Jedi force arrayed against him, but at that point Baas demanded that Qel-Droma cease his resistance and surrender. Telekinetically disarming both Qel-Droma and his Krath soldiers, the Jedi Master, aided by Sunrider and the other Jedi, used a [[Wall of light|Force technique]] to temporarily [[Prisoner/Legends|imprison]] the former Jedi inside a construct made of the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]]. Sidrona, expressing gratitude to Baas, then placed Qel-Droma under Republic jurisdiction and proclaimed that he would stand [[Trial of Ulic Qel-Droma|trial]]—and be sentenced to death—for his actions. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|Why did we fail? My warriors must know. Our honor demands it. What did we forget? How was my master Ulic so easily captured? Or did Aleema call a retreat ''before'' the tide had turned? Has she betrayed us?|Mandalore the Indomitable|}} [[File:TrialOfUlicQelDroma-TSW3.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Following the battle, Ulic Qel-Droma faced a Republic trial for his actions.]] Qel-Droma's capture by the Republic prevented the [[Dark Reaper]], a [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] built by Kun and deployed earlier in the war, from being delivered to Coruscant.[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] The Krath retreated to the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]], where Keto executed any of her subordinates who questioned her right to lead the cult. There, Mandalore analyzed the battle on Coruscant, seeking to understand why the Mandalorians had failed to protect the man they had sworn their allegiance to. In the process, Mandalore came to suspect Keto's treachery but did not confront her over it, instead proposing plans to rescue Qel-Droma. However, Keto was dismissive of any role the former Jedi could play in the future plans of the Krath and instead ordered the Mandalorian to assemble the Krath and Mandalorian forces for a double-feint [[Battle of Kemplex Nine|attack]] on the Kemplex Nine station, which she reasoned the Republic would have left defenseless due to previously only having viewed it as a target of a diversion. After carrying out his orders, Mandalore himself traveled to the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], where he informed Kun of Qel-Droma's capture and upcoming trial and convinced the Dark Lord to attempt a rescue of his apprentice. The Mandalorians' role in the attack on Coruscant was later noted by [[Vilnau Teupt]] in the keynote address "[[Industry, Honor, Savagery: Shaping the Mandalorian Soul]]" at [[Brentaal Academy]]'s [[Proceedings of Galactic Anthropology and History|412th Proceedings of Galactic Anthropology and History]] in [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]],''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' and [[Jedi historian]] [[Tionne Solusar]] mentioned Qel-Droma's capture in her [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] compendium ''[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force]]'', as well. The battle was also recorded in a [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] galactic history [[The Essential Chronology|chronicle]] by the [[Historical Council]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] as well as its [[36 ABY]] [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|updated edition]] by the Council's incarnation under the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]]. ==Behind the scenes== After being first alluded to as a planned event in the [[August 15]], [[1995]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54595/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_the_sith_war_1995_1|text=Star Wars: Tales Of The Jedi - The Sith War (1995) #1}} [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|first issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] mini-series, the Battle of Coruscant made its first appearance and was identified by name in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|next issue]], which was published on [[September 19]] of the same year.{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54596/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_the_sith_war_1995_2|text=Star Wars: Tales Of The Jedi - The Sith War (1995) #2}} Both comic issues were written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and penciled by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]] The [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry for Ulic Qel-Droma erroneously stated that it was Qel-Droma, not Mandalore the Indomitable, who wanted to capture the Galactic Republic weapons during the battle. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 1]] {{1stm}} {{Imo}}
